sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United have struggled in their last 5 matches, winning only 1 and losing 4, with a leaky defense conceding 11 goals. Crystal Palace, on the other hand, remain unbeaten in their last 5, securing 1 win and 4 draws, showing solid defensive organization with just 1 goal conceded in recent league games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results have been split with each team winning once away from home (Crystal Palace 2-0 at West Ham, and West Ham 2-0 at Palace). Historically, Crystal Palace have dominated with 3 wins in the last 5 matchups against West Ham.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham rank poorly in defense (20th for goals conceded) despite decent attacking stats like corners per game (5.75, 5th). Crystal Palace's conservative style is reflected in fewer goals but strong defensive metrics, conceding the fewest goals in recent matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Crystal Palace's current form and defensive stability give them an edge, while West Ham's home advantage and desperation for points could make it competitive. Jean-Philippe Mateta's threat (4 goals in last 5 head-to-heads) could be decisive for Palace.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Crystal Palace lead West Ham United by a single goal at the London Stadium. The scoreline stands at 0-1, with Jean-Philippe Mateta finding the net in the 37th minute with a headed effort. West Ham have struggled to create significant scoring opportunities, while Palace have looked more threatening in attack, capitalizing on their chance to take the lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United have dominated ball possession in the first half, maintaining a slight edge in territorial play with numerous entries into the final third. However, their inability to convert this dominance into clear chances has been evident, with minimal shots on target and no big chances created. Defensively, they've been somewhat stable but were caught out for Mateta’s goal. Crystal Palace, on the other hand, have been more clinical despite having less of the ball. Their higher number of penalty area entries and corner kicks suggest a more direct and dangerous approach in attack, and their defensive structure has held firm against West Ham’s pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. West Ham started brightly, controlling the early stages with consistent attacking spells and pushing forward with intent. However, as the half progressed, Crystal Palace gradually shifted the momentum in their favor, particularly in the middle of the half, with sustained periods of pressure that ultimately led to their goal. Towards the end of the half, Palace continued to look more threatening, while West Ham’s attacking impetus waned, struggling to regain control of the game’s rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For Crystal Palace, Jean-Philippe Mateta has been the standout performer, not only for his goal but also for his presence in the box and ability to win duels. Daichi Kamada has also impressed with his creativity and involvement in build-up play. On the West Ham side, James Ward-Prowse has shown glimpses of quality in midfield with key passes, while Konstantinos Mavropanos has been a defensive rock, making crucial clearances and contributing to their only notable shot on target. However, West Ham will need more from their forward line, particularly players like Jarrod Bowen and Callum Wilson, to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, West Ham United are likely to come out with renewed intensity as they chase an equalizer. Their higher possession stats suggest they have the capability to build pressure, but they must be more clinical in front of goal. Substitutions could play a key role, with fresh legs potentially needed to inject creativity. Crystal Palace, meanwhile, will likely aim to protect their lead while seeking opportunities on the counter-attack. Their ability to maintain defensive solidity and exploit West Ham’s desperation could be decisive. Expect a competitive half with both teams having chances to score.
